entry.title: The Sydney Morning Herald: 25 Jun, 2022
entry.summary: The Sydney Morning Herald: 25 Jun, 2022 published at 2022-06-25 07:08AM.
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Oslo shooting being investigated as terrorism
  • AFL 2022 round 15 LIVE updates: Fyfe makes umpire contact, Stewart in trouble for Prestia hit, Blues overrun Freo
  • Papali'i seals the game for NZ
  • What overturning Roe v Wade means for Australia
  • Ukrainians wake to renewed Russian assaults from several directions
  • How much more does Tupou need to do to prove he’s true Blue?
  • Fyfe faces umpire scrutiny as Blues overcome slow start to down Dockers
  • Cabarita house sold for $4.72 million to buyers who plan to knock it down
  • Gallery: The best photos from around the world
  • How training with his late brother in the backyard made Martin Queensland’s public enemy No.1
  • ‘He deserves this’: Trent Barrett breaks silence to praise top Dog Burton
  • Victorian man travelled to Sydney to meet girl under 10 for sex: police
  • Boasts of new era of domination could come back to haunt the Blues
  • Is a career as a horseracing peacekeeper next for Martin Pakula?
  • Kiwi young guns combine for magical try
  • ‘A Test 100 in India’: Maxwell lays out his credentials for Sri Lanka
  • Your weekly horoscope June 26-July 3
  • Greens must remember why they hold balance of power
  • Jezebels deliver an otherworldly trip back to the summer of 2011
  • Ex-Mongols bikie Sam Abdulrahim shot leaving Melbourne funeral
  • Best of cartoons, June 25, 2022
  • Rising interest rates, inflation give Lowe a challenging high wire act
  • First cuts in October budget even as companies shower money on bottom line
  • The latest illustrations from artist Michael Leunig
  • The latest illustrations from artist Matt Golding
  • The latest illustrations from artist Simon Letch
  • Best of cartoons, June 24, 2022
  • The latest illustrations from artist Alan Moir
  • Crossbench fury as government cuts staffers from four to one
  • Urban rainforest, ocean pools: 10 ideas to transform Circular Quay
  • Top of the heap: why Barilaro’s the perfect choice to be our man in New York
  • Sydney limo driver ‘couldn’t cope with the stress’ after sparking COVID outbreak, lockdown
  • Raising kids is a job for parents, not teachers
  • Thousands gather to celebrate 44th anniversary of first Mardi Gras
  • Don’t treat voters like fools when it comes to political appointments
  • Baby killer Kathleen Folbigg seeks government funding for new inquiry
  • Sydney train disruptions expected despite urgent talks between union, ministers
  • Plan for plunge pool at headmaster’s residence at King’s fuels tension
  • Germany is finding it’s not easy being green
  • Monkeypox virus spreading, showing more mutations than expected
  • Boris Johnson’s losing streak follows him all the way to Kigali
  • US Supreme Court wipes out constitutional right to abortion, state bans come into place
  • Abortion rights under threat in US
  • The centre of the war in Ukraine
  • Problems at home? Get to Kyiv and be photographed with Zelensky
  • Congress sends landmark gun violence bill to Biden to sign into law
  • Wasteland warriors: The long, cruel summer on Ukraine’s eastern front
  • Australian-backed candidate fails to topple Commonwealth boss Patricia Scotland
  • Prince Charles tells Commonwealth nations ‘becoming a republic’ is up to them
  • January 6 panel receives new Trump documentary footage
  • Millionaire jailed after stealing Banksy’s Sad Girl
  • Maroons and Blues ready for Optus Stadium State of Origin clash
  • ‘I won’t go away until I get what I want’: Man blackmailed woman with intimate images
  • ‘Get here early’: Victorians endure airport anarchy, just to get out for winter
  • Frankston serial killer Paul Denyer could be out of prison from next year
  • Qld Labor MPs relent and release CCC’s legal costs in secretive Trad case
  • Vigil to be held for Queensland police officer Dave Masters on anniversary of tragic hit and run
  • Man arrested over alleged online grooming
  • Man charged over Yokine burglary and attempted sexual assault
  • PM set for feud with crossbench
  • Another day of travel chaos
  • Week of strike action for NSW
  • ‘We are in for a tough winter’: Sharon Lewin, the clear-eyed optimist
  • Ash, Dylan, Dusty: Mindset coach Ben Crowe explains how he makes our best better
  • Advice on fourth COVID shot for many Australians may be delayed
  • Nobbling Afterpay would stifle competition and protect bank profits
  • On the fast lane: Which occupations get the most ‘speeding’ fines?
  • (Book)dystopia: How Australia’s biggest bookseller went from hero to villain
  • Packer’s casino dream edges closer to reality - without him
  • Cosmos Clinics faces two class action investigations
  • Sydney’s inner-city set for sparkling rooftop pool in $200m project
  • Auctioneers make an art of selling property collector’s assets
  • Service with a smile: Virgin seeks to seize on Qantas' stumbles
  • ‘It will get smoother’: Slashing flights will reduce chaos, Qantas CEO Alan Joyce says
  • Here to stay or will they go away? Either way, NFTs have transformed Australian art
  • After everything was destroyed by floods, National Art School steps up for students
  • SBS drama about exploited delivery riders tries to hire extras without pay
  • Fallout continues from Lisa Wilkinson’s vocal Logies moment
  • The new Queensland buildings that ‘deliver with delight’
  • ‘Exceptional’: The whimsical self-portrait that won Australia’s newest portrait prize
  • Devil in disguise? Elvis film starts a little more conversation about his manager
  • From the screen to the jungle, Werner Herzog writes his first novel
  • The best home tech for your buck this tax time
  • Cold weather and power prices prompt record interest in batteries and solar

Breaking news from Sydney, Australia and the world. Features the latest business, sport, entertainment, travel, lifestyle, and technology news.

entry.title: Washington Post: 25 Jun, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Russia will soon exhaust its combat capabilities, Western assessments predict
  • Two killed in Oslo nightlife-district shooting on eve of Pride parade
  • U.S. abortion decision ‘horrific’ and ‘appalling,’ world leaders say
  • Diplomats urge action as global food crisis deepens
  • Ukraine retreats from Severodonetsk as Russia advances in the East
  • Taliban struggles to respond to earthquake amid international isolation
  • Ukraine pulls back from Severodonetsk
  • Indigenous protesters are paralyzing Ecuador. Here’s why.
  • Is U.S. a liberal outlier on abortion, as Supreme Court opinion says?
  • Boris Johnson’s Tories suffer fresh electoral defeat; party chair quits
  • As Biden heads to Europe, the mood on Ukraine is grimmer
  • In many countries, abortion is protected by law, not court decision
  • U.N. rights body says Israeli soldiers killed American journalist in West Bank
  • Britain to pull $18B in bank notes as it switches from paper to polymer
  • They wanted rule of law, so China tried them in secret
  • 2 killed in mass shooting in Norway; more than a dozen hurt
  • With war, Kyiv pride parade becomes a peace march in Warsaw
  • US abortion ruling sparks global debate, polarizes activists
  • Ecuador president: Indigenous leader is trying to stage coup
  • Ukrainian army leaving battered city for fortified positions
  • Donors pledge $160 million, Palestinian refugees need more
  • Mexico climber dies scaling active, off-limits volcano
  • Migrant caravan in south Mexico demands corridor to border
  • Indigenous and family mourn expert killed in the Amazon
  • Dissident artist, rapper sentenced to prison in Cuba
  • Biden says restoring abortion rights is up to voters
  • Protests erupt in D.C., around the country as Roe v. Wade falls
  • Women of color will be most impacted by the end of Roe, experts say
  • No matter if one feels relief or devastation, the weight of overturning Roe is profound
  • Roe’s demise marks new phase in state-by-state battle over abortion
  • Susan Collins says testimony by Gorsuch, Kavanaugh ‘inconsistent’ with ruling
  • LGBTQ community braces for rollback of rights after abortion ruling
  • Abortion advocates fear access to birth control could be curtailed
  • The scene outside the Supreme Court after Roe v. Wade was overturned
  • ‘Still a nightmare’: Surfside families mark one year since collapse
  • Pence calls for national abortion ban as Trump, GOP celebrate end of Roe
  • Tex. school district bans dresses, skirts to promote ‘workforce skills’
  • Tell The Post: How do you feel about the Supreme Court’s abortion ruling?
  • 6,000 bees moved into a century-old home. Savvy beekeepers evicted them.
  • J.K. Rowling thought she was talking to Zelensky. It was Russian pranksters.
  • They went hiking to get closer to God. Instead, they had to be rescued.
  • Jan. 6 committee, DOJ seek footage of Roger Stone from Danish filmmakers
  • She ordered a burger on Grubhub — and included a desperate plea for help
  • Oath Keepers’ defense ordered to disclose if Sidney Powell is funding attorneys
  • Post Politics Now: House poised to send gun legislation to Biden in response to mass shootings
  • Intelligence contractor, alleged Nazi sympathizer charged in Jan. 6 riot
  • Congress extends pandemic-era school meal program, with caveats
  • Disney says it will cover employee travel costs for abortions
  • Dow soars more than 800 points as Wall Street ends week on high note
  • How women’s lives were different before Roe v. Wade
  • Crypto hackers steal $100 million from blockchain bridge
  • This tax season, IRS answered just 10 percent of taxpayer calls
  • What is a recession? Your economy questions, answered.
  • 'Bring rifles’: Extremist groups call for violence over abortion ruling
  • Video game industry on the end of Roe: I’m ‘not proud to be an American’
  • For people seeking abortions, digital privacy is suddenly critical
  • Multigenerational, ADA-compliant house lists for $2.2M
  • Britain’s Summer of Discontent Is Thrashing Conservatives
  • North Korean Hackers Have Crypto In Their Crosshairs
  • Can Companies Still Cover Abortion Travel Costs?
  • Bond Traders Are Reading the Federal Reserve Wrong Again
  • What are War Bonds and Why Did Ukraine Sell Them?

  • The Supreme Court found a way to make our gun problem worse
  • The Supreme Court’s radical abortion ruling begins a dangerous new era
  • Will Biden run for president again? 12 of our columnists have the answer.
  • The court’s abortion ruling pours gasoline on our culture-war fires
  • The radical conservative majority’s damage to the Supreme Court cannot be undone
  • Why the Supreme Court’s gun ruling is an entirely reasonable one
  • Flawed reasoning in gun ruling shows how Breyer will be missed
  • We must protect life from conception until the moment of birth!
  • The Supreme Court guts gun laws, while Congress finally acts
  • For the fall of Roe v. Wade, thank Donald Trump
  • The Post’s endorsements in the Democratic primary for Montgomery County Council
  • The Supreme Court eviscerates abortion rights and its own legitimacy
  • We know what to do to save the giant sequoias
  • The Supreme Court takes away a woman’s autonomy over her body
  • The first domino
  • Europe’s youngest democracy is a new front in the U.S.-Russia battle of ideas
  • Far too little has changed in Maryland since our first gun violence protest
  • End the tipped wage to help back-of-house staff
  • Now is the time for moral courage, not more guns
  • Protect the Potomac
  • Protecting speech and ensuring safety in Virginia after Dobbs
  • The Texas antiabortion law in action

Breaking news and analysis on politics, business, world national news, entertainment more. In-depth DC, Virginia, Maryland news coverage including traffic, weather, crime, education, restaurant reviews and more.

CNA: Breaking News, Singapore News, World and Asia

entry.title: South China Morning Post: 25 Jun, 2022
entry.summary: South China Morning Post: 25 Jun, 2022 published at 2022-06-25 06:00AM.
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Coronavirus: Hong Kong hotels raise rates for quarantine rooms as number of arrivals to city outpaces available spots
  • Immigrant who swam to Hong Kong and dropped out of school to work becomes highly qualified chef honoured by Japan
  • Hong Kong’s developers step up to fill the shortage in quality pension homes for the golden years in the world’s fastest-ageing population
  • Xi Jinping to attend 25th anniversary celebrations of Hong Kong’s return to Chinese sovereignty and swearing-in of new city leader John Lee
  • Hong Kong’s Asian expats still see city as a ‘land of opportunities’
  • How Hong Kong’s redevelopment projects can be showcases of ‘organic city’ growth
  • Hong Kong customs seizes 3.8 tonnes of date rape drug GBL in first half of 2022, dwarfing amount detected over past decade
  • Cracks in first-floor flat hanging above passers-by in Hong Kong spark calls for inspection of nearby structures
  • First phase of new cable works completed, Hong Kong power firm says, days after fire from high-voltage lines caused widespread blackouts
  • Hong Kong Book Fair to begin on July 20 but some publishers banned with ‘no reason’
  • Hong Kong court orders European firm to pay HK$150,000, apologise to former employee sacked over sexual harassment claim
  • Hong Kong customs officers arrest 5 men after seizure of HK$4.5 million worth of frozen meat from ship
  • Nansha tech boost offers a bigger cake for ‘brother’ Hong Kong, not rivalry: Guangzhou mayor
  • Hong Kong chauffeur acquitted on charge of inciting members of drivers’ WhatsApp group to attack police with machetes
  • Why Hong Kong’s new textbooks for secondary schools have stopped calling the city a British colony
  • The Age of Health Misinformation: From Pandemic to Infodemic
  • Passing on Lessons from the Field
  • Discovering a Passion for Literature
  • Immigration: the Dream vs the Facts
  • Following Friends in Fitness Apps is Associated with Higher Levels of Physical Activity
  • Hardening National Boundaries in a Globally-connected World
  • Why do Some Minorities Support the US Republican Party?
  • Into the World of Eileen Chang
  • Exploring Global Connectivity and Opportunities along Belt and Road
  • Changing the World for the Better
  • Chinese military says US plane in Taiwan Strait endangered peace
  • China’s space station may get the most precise clock in orbit – if it passes key test
  • China’s live-streaming e-commerce offers lifeline to fruit farmers amid Covid restrictions and short season
  • A bridge too far? Taipei mayor draws fire for proposal to link Quemoy and Xiamen
  • China jobs: facing lean times, small businesses dial back summer hiring
  • China ‘no-limits’ vow with Russia raises Pentagon urgency to prepare for Guam attack: US commander
  • Scientists unravel structure of ocean currents in the South China Sea
  • Chinese satellites evade US surveillance probe, then stare back, according to report
  • Chinese-hosted BRICS summit swerves controversy over Ukraine and Taiwan
  • China maps out digital government plan to 2035 as Beijing taps technology to improve its overall efficiency and control
  • Xinjiang ‘forced labour’ law leaves Chinese companies in a bind, with doubts over supply chain audits
  • Close Xi Jinping ally appointed as China’s new public security chief
  • Covid-19 in China: Woman and ailing father threatened with detention after clash with police while trying to visit hospital
  • Chinese firm aiming to break US dominance in chip design software gets IPO approval at home
  • China, US shipping rates continue to tumble as inflation cools consumer demand
  • China’s internet watchdog launches cybersecurity investigation into country’s largest academic research database CNKI
  • China’s new live-streaming guidelines set to change the influencer business known for its ‘low threshold, high income’
  • ‘See you next year’: China’s ‘King of gaokao’ fails to get marks for dream university after sitting the entrance exam for 26th time
  • Chinese security official calls for crackdown on gangs following Tangshan attack
  • China’s 20th party congress: former political star Lu Hao named to head key policy think tank
  • Why is a South Korean fringe group backing Japan’s position on WWII ‘comfort women’?
  • US, Japan and allies form new Pacific group to blunt China’s influence
  • Philippines’ Duterte leaves brutal legacy in presidency marked by drug war, US-China policy flip-flops
  • 6 arrested in Indonesia for blasphemy after bar’s free drinks for Muhammad promo backfires
  • Malaysia 1MDB scandal: Blow for Najib as court allows co-accused Arul Kanda to testify against him in trial
  • Myanmar’s NUG says junta placed Aung San Suu Kyi in solitary confinement to ‘embarrass’ her
  • Roe vs Wade: How betting on Trump paid off for US anti-abortion supporters
  • Call reveals Russia’s Putin told Macron he ‘wanted to play ice hockey’ on eve of Ukraine invasion
  • Roe vs Wade: Judge in US abortion ruling signals gay marriage, contraception next in firing line
  • Mexico woman, 22, dies climbing off-limits active volcano
  • Friends at first sniff: people drawn to others who smell like them, study shows
  • US passes first major gun control bill in decades
  • Ukraine war highlights G7’s role as geopolitical linchpin
  • US Supreme Court overturns Roe vs Wade, ending constitutional right to abortion
  • Prince Charles says Commonwealth nations free to decide on dropping queen as head of state
  • UN says Al Jazeera journalist Shireen Abu Akleh killed by Israeli forces
  • Ukraine forces to retreat from battleground city Sievierodonetsk to avoid encirclement
  • World leaders seek united front for Ukraine at key summits as war rages
  • Sunac China may miss instalment payment on 4 billion yuan bond as lockdowns pile pressure on beleaguered Chinese developer
  • Sun Hung Kai Properties sells about 90 per cent of last units at Silicon Hill’s phase one amid strong buying interest
  • Federal Reserve’s quantitative tightening plan is a welcome dose of predictability
  • H&M closes Shanghai flagship store following lockdowns and consumer backlash
  • 33-year-old Chinese woman splashes US$84.2 million in cash on exclusive 49-room property in Sardinia
  • Hong Kong’s second SPAC zooms in on smart cars, supply chain, e-commerce as potential takeover targets
  • ‘Death of Hong Kong’ predictions were wrong in 1997 and are still off the mark today
  • CATL to supply Li Auto, Hozon electric car battery with a range of 1,000km as Chinese firm seeks to stay on top
  • Hong Kong billionaire Henry Cheng’s family aims to take control of apparel retailer Giordano
  • Chef Samuel Lee Sum on being a Hongkonger in Paris, passing a test ‘worse than Top Chef or MasterChef’, and where to eat in the French capital
  • Kingdom star Ju Ji-hoon eyed for new sci-fi drama Dominant Species; Cho Seung-woo set to play pianist turned lawyer in Sacred Divorce – K-drama casting latest
  • Netflix Korean reality show Change Days, hosted by comedian Jang Do-yeon, gets couples to break up with each other
  • How aromatherapy can benefit your mind and body – and how essential oils help with cancer patients’ recovery
  • A US bakery tried to trademark mochi muffins and failed – rightfully. Not every food creation needs to be trademarked
  • How to manage teenage acne: simple skincare is key, says expert, as is sunscreen and consistency
  • Down in one: the origin of shooters, how they and their non-PC names are still part of bar culture, and where to get them in Hong Kong
  • The risks and benefits of diet trends on Instagram and TikTok, as explained by an expert
  • 5 child stars who survived financial meltdowns: from Britney Spears’ 13-year conservatorship to Lindsay Lohan’s rehab stint and Macaulay Culkin’s trust fund
  • Inside Elon Musk and first wife Justine Wilson’s murky marriage: their trans daughter Vivian Jenna Wilson just ditched his name for her mum’s, but what was their pre-fame relationship really like?
  • Meet James Wilkie Broderick, son of Sarah Jessica Parker and Matthew Broderick – from his SATC fashion icon mum’s down-to-earth parenting style to his possible future music career
  • Who’s the richest Presley? Elvis and family’s net worths, ranked: from Priscilla’s Graceland millions and Lisa-Marie’s tax debts, to Caitlyn Jenner’s ex Linda Thompson and final fiancée Ginger Alden
  • Meet Jennifer Lopez’s teen son, Max Muñiz: his twin sibling Emme, who came out as queer this week, is a singer – but he’s already made his acting debut and bonds with Ben Affleck over films
SCMP.com, Hong Kong's premier online English daily provides exclusive up-to-date news, audio video news, podcasts, RSS Feeds, Blogs, breaking news, top stories, award winning news and analysis on Hong Kong and China.

entry.title: Asahi Shimbun: 25 Jun, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Teen pair get mayor to set aside 100 million yen to upgrade facilities
  • Lost USB devices with data of all Amagasaki residents found
  • Sources: Russia likely identifies 2 bodies from sunken Kazu I
  • 30% of students in 3rd year of junior high have poor eyesight
  • Okinawans pray for peace amid growing tensions abroad, at home
  • Who owns what: World’s nuclear warheads count at a glance
  • City works with orchestra in new El Sistema music program for kids
  • Survey: 68% of candidates favor bolstering Japan’s defense
  • Survey: Coalition to win majority of Upper House seats contested
  • U.S. Pacific admiral: Ties with Japan key to regional security
  • CPI increases by more than 2% for second straight month
  • SMBC Nikko’s internal probe decries ‘unjust acts’ by execs
  • Exec returns to Toyota as adviser after 2015 arrest in Japan
  • Toyota recalls first mass-produced EVs less than 2 months after launch
  • China envoy says Australia fired first shot with Huawei ban
  • Yellen to visit South Korea to discuss sanctions against North
  • North Korea approves new frontline army duties amid tensions
  • Afghanistan ends search for survivors of earthquake that killed 1,000
  • Taiwan donates $1 million for Afghan quake relief efforts
  • Hong Kongers reflect on Taiwan, an imperfect exile
  • Myanmar says Suu Kyi held alone in new prison quarters
  • Ukrainian army leaving battered city for fortified positions
  • Supreme Court overturns Roe v. Wade; states can ban abortion
  • US abortion ruling sparks global debate, polarizes activists
  • TPNW meeting statement bashes states under nuclear umbrella
  • U.S. sending advanced rocket systems, other aid to Ukraine
  • European Union makes Ukraine a candidate for EU membership
  • Study: For good health, don’t stay up later than 2 hours on days off
  • Kyoto temple shows cultural treasures from new storage area
  • EDITORIAL: Security strategy should not rely on military power alone
  • VOX POPULI: Recovery of missing flash drive simply a very lucky break
  • VOX POPULI: Self-justification renders Tokyo Olympics report cringeworthy
The Asahi Shimbun is widely regarded for its journalism as the most respected daily newspaper in Japan. The English version offers selected articles from the vernacular Asahi Shimbun, as well as extensive coverage of cool Japan,focusing on manga, travel and other timely news.

The Korea JoongAng Daily is an English-language daily published by the JoongAng Group, Korea’s leading media group, in association with The New York Times.
